
The stacks project defines morphism $f_\bullet : A_\bullet \to B_\bullet$ of filtered vector spaces as strict if $f_i(A_i) = f_0(A_0) \cap B_i$. Rewriting this, we see it asks $\mathrm{im} f_\bullet = Q(\mathrm{im} f_0 : \mathrm{im} )$
<iframe class="quiver-embed" src="https://q.uiver.app/#&embed" width="700" height="200" style="border-radius: 8px; border: none; filter: invert(1);"></iframe>
An alternative, more general definition, says that a morphism $f : A \to B$ of a preabelian category is strict if $\mathrm{im} f \cong \mathrm{coim} f$. Does the latter definition imply the former?
